conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex who is 4 years old, and Blake who is 14.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! 

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to ask you some questions.

First, are you responsible for Alex and Blake? This means do they normally live with you, or do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit to their upkeep (even if they don't live with you)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake lives with me at home. But Alex doesn't live with me - Alex lives with their other parent. However, I do contribute money towards Alex's upkeep regularly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Regarding Alex - you mentioned you contribute money towards their upkeep regularly. Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (which is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child, and £16.95 per week for additional children) towards Alex's upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute more than that. I help with clothes, school things, and other expenses for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Now, does anyone else currently claim Child Benefit for Alex? For example, does Alex's other parent (who Alex lives with) claim Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation and immigration status.

Do you live in the UK, and are you either:
- A UK citizen, or
- Have the right to live in the UK (for example, through settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Are Alex and Blake both under 16 years old? (I know you mentioned Alex is 4 and Blake is 14, so I believe they are, but I want to confirm). Also, are either of them in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is 4 and Blake is 14, so they're both under 16. Alex is in nursery and Blake is in secondary school, just normal school for their ages.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information.

Just one final question: Is there anyone else who might also be responsible for Blake (who lives with you)? For example, does Blake have another parent or guardian who also lives with Blake or contributes to their upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake lives here with me. Their other parent doesn't live with us and doesn't contribute to Blake's upkeep - I'm the one looking after Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I have good news!

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:

**For Blake (age 14):**
Blake lives with you, is under 16, and you are responsible for them. Since Blake's other parent doesn't live with you or contribute to Blake's upkeep, you meet the eligibility requirements as the person responsible for Blake.

**For Alex (age 4):**
Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you are still eligible because you contribute more than the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep through clothes, school expenses, and other costs. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so you can claim it.

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. As Blake lives with you, Blake would be considered your eldest/only child living with you for the purposes of calculating the benefit amount.

To apply, you'll need to complete the Child Benefit claim form. You may need to provide evidence of your contributions towards Alex's upkeep when you apply.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 4 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old (under 16), and although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant contributes more than the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep (contributing towards clothes, school things, and other expenses). The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you can claim Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child, which includes contributing at least the same as Child Benefit to their upkeep even if they don't live with you. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 14 years old (under 16) and lives with the claimant. The claimant is responsible for Blake, and Blake's other parent does not live with them or contribute to their upkeep. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK. Blake is in secondary school (normal education for their age). According to the rules, you can claim Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with two children: Alex (age 4) and Blake (age 14). Blake lives with the claimant who is solely responsible for them. Alex lives with their other parent, but the claimant contributes more than the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets the eligibility criteria for both children.
